channel.title: Blog on Christian Mahnke
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on Christian Mahnke

item.title: "Liebespaar" (Lovers) by Max Westhäuser (1885-1956)
item.description: I have acquired a colour woodcut by Max Westhäuser (1885-1956)…

item.title: JavaScript library for HDR
item.description: The JavaScript implementation for the last posts is now also available as NPM module.

item.title: TTS for "Briefsteller"
item.description: After the Briefsteller blog had existed as a skeleton since the beginning of 2021, I used my summer holiday to bring the sample letter, which brought me to the topic “letter writing guides” in the first place, online…

item.title: Siedelhof Jena by Curt Mücke (1885-1940)
item.description: I found an colourised etching by Curt Mücke (1885-1940)…

item.title: Contrast enhancement for UV images using HDR
item.description: This example is about a practical use case for HDR visualisation in the browser…

item.title: LibUltraHDR
item.description: After research, it was necessary to explore some possible workflows and to create tools for a project in preparation. Based on Greg Benz’s preliminary work on HDR support, LibUltraHDR was the most sensible candidate for further experimentation.

item.title: Sheet by Imao Keinen (1855-1924)
item.description: I bought a Japanese colour woodcut by Imao Keinen (1855-1924)…

item.title: Data Containers
item.description: Even if it is not necessarily the intended use case, Docker makes it very easy to package large amounts of data with applications for simplified provisioning (such as APIs). This can be particularly helpful if, for example, standardisation data from different sources is to be merged for data enrichment.

item.title: DIY Project: Pong Visualisation
item.description: For some reason I came up with the idea of visualising Pong…

item.title: Etching byKurt Mühlenhaupt (1921-2006)
item.description: I found an etching by Kurt Mühlenhaupt (1921-2006)…

item.title: VintageReality launched
item.description: The latest blog incubated at the Projektemacher Labs has been started.

item.title: 3D Models in the browser
item.description: Maybe it will become a virtual exhibition about orange ceramics…

item.title: Etching by Marie Gey-Heinze (1881-1908)
item.description: I have acquired an etching by Marie Gey-Heinze (1881-1908)…

item.title: Mystery Box
item.description: I bought a (more or less) mysterious box:

item.title: Abstrakte Grafik von Hans Martin Erhardt (1935-2015)
item.description: I have acquired a linocut by Hans Martin Erhardt (1935-2015)…

item.title: Reisen wie 1996
item.description: Sometimes you wonder how old the systems in trains are, here the passenger information system of an ICE 1 with an AMD 5x86 P-75 S CPU.

item.title: Windows Deployer
item.description:
How to install a freshly built software from GitLab / GitHub?
On a Windows computer.
In an internal network - behind a VPN gateway

item.title: Holzschnitt von Erich Zimmer (1908-2001)
item.description: I have purchased a woodcut by Erich Zimmer (1908-2001)…

item.title: Puppen by Elsa Hoppe (1901-1992)
item.description: I have acquired a linocut by Elsa Hoppe (1901-1992)…

item.title: FarbholzschnittColour woodblock print by Oscar Droege (1898-1983)
item.description: I have found another colour woodcut by Oscar Droege (1898-1983)…

item.title: GeoIP Tool
item.description: Kibana visualisation with room plans needs data, e.g. from devices in a building. But the data has to come from somewhere…

item.title: OAI-PMH Harvester
item.description: I didn’t want to write the millionth OAI-PMH harvester….

item.title: Holzschnitt von Eva Roemer (1989-1977)
item.description: I found a woodcut by Eva Roemer (1989-1977)…

item.title: Hildesheimer Börde
item.description: The rest stop “Hildesheimer Börde” on a foggy night.

item.title: Maps for my Projektemacher blogs
item.description: Another addition from Projektemacher Labs: Preparations Before posts can be displayed on maps, the following steps must be taken: Geocoding of posts and / or tags Generation of an application-specific representations (GeoJSON, KML) After this has been done, the next step can be approached. An example of the old 3D images can be found here. Plotting data on a map in a browser The map material provided by OpenStreetMap can be used to display the generated data in the browser.

item.title: Donation to the city archive Halle
item.description: Another donation to an archive should have arrived by now in city archives of Halle an der Saale. It concerns three postcards, the outlets of the Stadtgut Gimritz.

item.title: Exhibition "Wilma Bräuner"
item.description:
From December 6, the exhibition “Spurensuche im Sylter Archiv - Zum Leben und Werk von Wilma Bräuner (1891 - 1985)”, on which Evelyn Kraßmann and I worked together with a team, will take place in the city gallery “Alte Post”, Stephanstraße 4, Sylt / OT Westerland. The web page is still under construction….

The vernissage will take place on Monday, December 5, 2022 at 6 pm in the Stadtgalerie.

item.title: Hang pictures in Sweet Home 3D
item.description: For the planning of an exhibition using a 3D program, I have been looking into how pre-arranged picture frames could be imported into Sweet Home 3D…

item.title: Projektemacher Merch started
item.description: Even though some of the designs are older, the time has finally come:

item.title: IIIF Proxy
item.description: To improve the ‘displayability’ of digital objects such as coins and books, I have written a proxy for IIIF resources…

item.title: IIIF Conference 2021: Scaling down...
item.description: Today I gave my talk about the infrastructure of my blogs at this year’s IIIF conference.

item.title: Instagram
item.description: As of today there is an additional Instagram channel. This is just an experiment for now. But what has already annoys me me: There doesn’t seem to be an official and stable API for creating posts / uploading images - very disappointing!

item.title: DIY Project: Phone case
item.description: Today, once again, one of the rather rare non-digital projects: A cover for my new phone.

item.title: Briefsteller.de startet
item.description: Finally, the last of the blogs stared during the second Corona Lockdown is now online!

item.title: JPEG XL
item.description: Why JPEG XL? JPEG XL is a new format for raster images, in fact, is’s so recent (at the time this text was written) there haven’t even been an entry in the German Wikipedia. You can read there what exactly JPEG XL does differently or better than the well-known JPEG. We limit ourselves here to the advantages for the Projektemacher blogs: Less storage space required Lossless compression available Disadvantages are currently:

item.title: IIIF Konferenz 2021
item.description: I will present my Corona projects at this year’s IIIF conference!

item.title: Labs.projektemache.org gestartet
item.description: In order to have a page in the future that can easily break for a few days and to be able to present some things half-finished, there is now the Projektemacher Labs.

item.title: Living room renovated
item.description: In addition to my blogs, I used the corona period to renovate my living room. Here, too, I was able to realise some ideas that were years old: Put up striped historical wallpaper from Zeitzeug Rearrange my library Here are the individual steps:

item.title: Der erste Beitrag
item.description:
After the site Projektemacher.org went online in the last few months to unite my old Tumblr blogs and the projects of the first Corona Lockdown under a single

one roof as an umbrella brand, mine is now starting personal homepage.
